name: AWS Certified Machine Learning Engineer – Associate (MLA-C01)
description: Associate-level certification for ML engineers building and deploying ML solutions on AWS. Validates ability to implement ML pipelines, select models, deploy and monitor solutions. Experience with ML on AWS recommended. 85 questions, 170 minutes.
exportIntroQuestions:
## About this document

This document is a collection of practice exam questions for the **AWS Certified Machine Learning Engineer – Associate (MLA-C01)** certification. The questions were selected because they represent realistic ML engineering scenarios commonly encountered when building and deploying machine learning solutions on AWS.

### Certification context

This certification validates the ability to build, train, deploy, and monitor ML solutions on AWS, including topics such as:

* Preparing and engineering data for ML workloads (feature stores, data quality)
* Selecting and training ML models (SageMaker, Bedrock, built-in algorithms)
* Deploying models to production (real-time endpoints, batch inference)
* Orchestrating end-to-end ML pipelines (SageMaker Pipelines, Step Functions)
* Monitoring model performance, detecting drift, and ensuring security

### About the questions

Each question in this document is independent and should be analyzed as a standalone scenario. Practice exams intentionally present isolated situations, and questions may appear in any order during the actual certification exam. Information from one question should never be assumed to apply to another.

For each scenario, this document includes:

* The original practice exam question.
* An explanation of the ML engineering reasoning behind the correct answer.
* A discussion of why the remaining options are less appropriate for the stated requirements.

### Learning objective

The primary goal of this material is to develop ML engineering reasoning rather than memorization. The focus should be on understanding the data, model, and deployment requirements presented in each scenario, identifying the AWS ML services and patterns that best satisfy those requirements, and recognizing the trade-offs between accuracy, latency, cost, and operational complexity.

By studying the decision-making process behind each answer, readers can build the ML engineering skills expected for the AWS certification exam.
exportIntroTranscripts:
## About this document

This document contains technical summaries of lesson transcripts related to the **AWS Certified Machine Learning Engineer – Associate (MLA-C01)** certification.

### Purpose
Organizes and synthesizes lesson content in a structured format for quick review and deeper understanding of the concepts presented.
exportIntroChat:
## About this document

This document contains AI study conversations related to the **AWS Certified Machine Learning Engineer – Associate (MLA-C01)** certification.

### Purpose
Records discussions and explanations generated during interactive AI study sessions, serving as complementary reference material.

domains.name: Data Preparation for ML
domains.description: Domain 1 (28%): Ingest and store data for ML workloads, transform and engineer features, ensure data quality and governance for ML.

domains.name: ML Model Development
domains.description: Domain 2 (26%): Choose modeling approach (SageMaker, Bedrock), train and evaluate ML models, select and refine models.

domains.name: Deployment and Orchestration of ML Workflows
domains.description: Domain 3 (22%): Select deployment infrastructure (endpoints, batch), create and script ML pipelines, orchestrate end-to-end workflows.

domains.name: ML Solution Monitoring, Maintenance, and Security
domains.description: Domain 4 (24%): Monitor model performance (drift, bias), maintain ML solutions, secure ML workloads.
